subitems.title: Electoral Board Meeting May 12, 2026
August 4, 2026 Primary

meetings.raw_text: AGENDA Electoral Board Meeting Tuesday, June 2, 2026, 5:00 pm Office of Voter Registration and Elections Fredericksburg City Center Public Speakers The Electoral Board provides this opportunity for comments from voters before the start of the meeting. Comments relevant to the registration of voters, the establishment of voting precincts, and polling places, and the conduct of the elections are welcome. To be fair to everyone, please observe the five-minute time limit and yield the floor when the Chair of the Electoral Board indicates that your time has expired. Observers At this point the Board will Open the Meeting. All speakers and those observers who have waived their right to speak are reminded that at this point you are clearly and only observers. You are not invited to interrupt nor attempt to engage Board members in discussion of Board Agenda items or other business. A. Public Comment B. Approval of the Agenda C. Approval of Minutes a. Electoral Board Meeting May 12, 2026 D. General Registrar Report E. Old Business F. New Business a. August 4, 2026 Primary G. Closed Session H. Certification of Closed Session I. Next Meeting Date J. Adjournment 601 Caroline Street Suite 500 Fredericksburg, VA 22401 Email: vote@vote.fredericksburgva.gov Web: www.fredericksburgVA.gov/Vote Telephone: 540-372-1030 Fax: 540-373-8381

of the meeting. Comments relevant to the registration of voters, the establishment of

meetings.raw_text: Agenda - 05/27/2026 PLANNING COMMISSION AGENDA Wednesday, May 27, 2026 6:30 P.M. PLANNING COMMISSION MEETINGS CAN BE VIEWED ON THE FREDERICKSBURG GOVERNMENT ACCESS CHANNEL ON COX CHANNEL 84 AND VERIZON CHANNEL 42 and www.regionalwebtv.com/fredpc . 1. Call to Order 2. Pledge Of Allegiance 3. Determination of a Quorum 4. Approval of Agenda 4.A. Agenda Documents: 2026-05-27 Agenda.pdf 5. Discussion of Potential Policies, Ordinances or Applications 6. Declaration of Conflict of Interest and Disclosures of Contact 7. Old Business 8. Public Hearing Any person may speak at a public hearing for up to five minutes. An applicant in a land use case may give an opening presentation of the case for up to 15 minutes, regardless of the number of speakers the applicant chooses to give the presentation, and may give a response after all citizens have spoken, for up to five minutes, regardless of the number of speakers the applicant chooses to give the response. The Chairman may suspend these rules in order to adapt them to any special situation. Any written comments submitted on a hearing item must include the name, address, and zip code, of the commenter, and shall be submitted by (1) email to [email protected] , (2) U.S. Mail addressed to: 601 Caroline Street, Suite 400, Fredericksburg, VA 22401, or (3) delivered to the Community Planning and Building office located at 601 Caroline Street - 4th Floor by 2:30 PM the day of the meeting. Any written comments received will be forwarded to the Planning Commission and are available for public inspection upon request. Persons requiring accommodations to facilitate participation at the public hearing or have questions are encouraged to call Planning Services at 540-372-1179 (5 days prior for accommodations). 8.A. Area 8 Dixon Street / Mayfield Small Area Plan Comprehensive Plan Amendment and Zoning Map Amendment. COMPPLAN2605-0004 / ZMA2605-0014 Comprehensive Plan Amendment to Chapters 10 and 11 of the 2025 Comprehensive Plan to adopt a Small Area Plan for Planning Area 8 Zoning Map Amendment to rezone 44 parcels, encompassing approximately 33.14 acres of land located in Planning Area 8, currently zoned as Residential-4, I-1 Light Industrial, and Commercial Highway, to the Creative Maker District and to apply form-based regulations, as recommended in the draft Small Area Plan for Area 8 Documents: 1 Staff Memo - Area 8 Small Area Plan.pdf 2 Draft Small Area 8 Comprehensive Plan Amendment.pdf 3 Appendix 1- Changes Outside of Small Area 8.pdf 4 Zoning Map Amendment Map.pdf 5 Transect and Frontage Map.pdf 6 Area 8 Adopted Character Structures2.pdf 9. Other Business 9.A. Transmittal of UMW Presentation – “From Restriction to Resilience” Documents: From Restriction to Resilience - UMW Presentation.pdf 9.B. Transportation Project Overview Presentation 10. General Public Comment A general public comment period is provided at each regular meeting for comments by citizens regarding any matter related to Commission business that is not listed on the agenda for Public Hearing. The Chair will request that speakers state their name, address and zip code; observe the five-minute time limit; and yield the floor when the Clerk indicates that their time has expired. No dialogue between speakers will be permitted. Citizens who wish to make general public comments without attending the meeting will be able to send their comments in writing by (1) delivering to the Community Planning and Building office located at 601 Caroline Street - 4th Floor, (2) U.S. Mail at 601 Caroline Street, Suite 400, Fredericksburg, VA 22401, or (3) email to [email protected] . Comments must be received by 2:30 p.m. the day of the meeting. In written public comments, the writer must identify themselves by name and address, including zip code. Such comments will be available for public inspection upon request. Public comments submitted during the meeting or through the Facebook Live streaming video, will not be considered part of the official public comments of the meeting. 11. Approval Of Minutes 11.A. March 11, 2026 Draft Minutes Regular Meeting Documents: 2026-03-11 Draft Minutes Regular Meeting.pdf 11.B. April 3, 2026 Draft Minutes - CIP Committee Documents: 2026-04-03 Draft Minutes - CIP Committe.pdf 12. Planning Commissioner Comment 12.A. Chairman Comments 12.B. Commissioner Comments 13. Planning Director Comment 13.A. Legislative Updates 13.B. William Street Corridor Public Meeting – Wednesday, June 17, 6 pm-7:30 pm @ Dorothy Hart Community Center, 408 Canal Street, Fredericksburg, VA 22401 14. Adjournment The next Planning Commission Regular Meeting is scheduled for Wednesday, June 10, 2026, at 6:30 p.m. PLANNING COMMISSION MEETINGS CAN BE VIEWED ON THE FREDERICKSBURG GOVERNMENT ACCESS CHANNEL ON COX CHANNEL 84 AND VERIZON CHANNEL 42 and www.regionalwebtv.com/fredpc .

A general public comment period is provided at each regular meeting for comments by citizens regarding any matter related to Commission business that is not listed on the agenda for Public Hearing.  The Chair will request that speakers state their name, address and zip code; observe the five-minute time limit; and yield the floor when the Clerk indicates that their time has expired.  No dialogue between speakers will be permitted.

Citizens who wish to make general public comments without attending the meeting will be able to send their comments in writing by (1) delivering to the Community Planning and Building office located at 601 Caroline Street - 4th Floor, (2) U.S. Mail at 601 Caroline Street, Suite 400, Fredericksburg, VA 22401, or (3) email to

meetings.raw_text: Agenda - 05/26/2026 City of Fredericksburg, Virginia Hon. Kerry P. Devine, Mayor Hon. Charlie L. Frye, Jr., Vice-Mayor, Ward Four Hon. Jannan W. Holmes, At-Large Hon. Dr. Will B. Mackintosh, At-Large Hon. Matt D. Rowe, Ward One Hon. Joy Y. Crump, Ward Two Hon. Susanna R. Finn, Ward Three City Council AGENDA Council Chambers 715 Princess Anne Street Fredericksburg, Virginia 22401 May 26, 2026 7:30 pm Mayor Kerry P. Devine, Presiding 1. Call to Order 2. Invocation Vice-Mayor Charlie L. Frye, Jr. 3. Pledge of Allegiance Councilor Jannan W. Holmes 4. Presentations 5. Consent Agenda A. Resolution 26-__, Second Read, Amending the FY 2026 Budget to Supplement Funding in the Children's Services Act Fund Documents: 5A Amending the FY 2026 Budget to Supplement CSA Funding 2nd Read.pdf B. Transmittal of Board and Commission Minutes Documents: EDA Minutes_02.09.2026.pdf EDA Minutes_03.09.2026.pdf EDA Minutes_04.13.2026.pdf 300th Minutes_01.07.2026.pdf 300th Minutes_02.04.2026.pdf 300th Minutes_03.04.2026.pdf APVD Minutes_03.09.2026.pdf APVD Minutes_04.13.2026.pdf APVD Minutes_03.09.2026 (Supplementary Meeting).pdf 6. Public Hearing People who wish to participate remotely in the public hearing may send their comments in writing by 1) U.S. Mail or 2) email to the Clerk of Council. Written comments must be received no later than 1:30 p.m. on the meeting date. Written comments intended for a public hearing must be clearly identified. Written comments received on time will be read aloud during the public hearing. These rules apply to public comments: the person must identify themself by name and address, including zip code, limit remarks to five (5) minutes for speakers attending in person and three (3) minutes for submitted letters (read aloud), and comments must address a topic of City business. Decorum in the Council Chambers will be maintained. Comments that are not relevant to City business and behavior that is disruptive are inappropriate and out of order. 7. Comments from the Public City Council provides this opportunity each regular meeting for comments from people who have signed up with the Clerk of Council to speak before the start of the meeting. To be fair to everyone, please observe the five-minute time limit and yield the floor when the Clerk of Council indicates that your time has expired. Decorum in the Council Chambers will be maintained. Comments that are not relevant to City business and behavior that is disruptive are inappropriate and out of order. People who wish to participate remotely in the public comment period may send their comments in writing by 1) U.S. Mail or 2) email to the Clerk of Council. Written comments must be received no later than 1:30 p.m. on the meeting date . Written comments received on time will be read aloud during the public comment portion of the City Council meeting. These rules apply to remote public comments: the person must identify themself by name and address, including zip code, and limit remarks to three (3) minutes for submitted letters (read aloud) with a 40-minute cap on read letters. 8. Council Agenda 9. Minutes A. Minutes Documents: 04-14-26 Regular Session Minutes Draft .pdf 10. Boards and Commission Appointments 11. City Manager Agenda A. Resolution 26-__, Adopting the Fiscal Year 2027 – Fiscal Year 2031 Capital Improvement Plan Documents: 11A Adopting the Fiscal Year 2027 _ Fiscal Year 2031 Capital Improvement Plan.pdf B. Resolution 26-__, Authorizing the Use of Surplus Motor Fuels Tax for Transportation Purposes in the FY 2026 Budget Documents: 11B Authorizing Use of 1,100,597 of Motor Fuels Tax Funds for Various Transportation Projects.pdf C. Calendars Documents: 11C Calendars.pdf 12. Adjournment

2026-05-27.summary: This joint work session allows city officials to review feasibility scenarios and economic impacts for the Phase III Commercial Corridor Revitalization Study. The discussion focuses on how specific development models for shopping centers might affect the local economy and urban planning goals. No binding votes are taken during this informational session; it is designed to gather input before formal decisions are made.
2026-05-27.why: Changes to commercial corridors directly influence the city's tax base by determining which businesses can operate in designated zones. These zoning adjustments affect local traffic patterns, public safety resource allocation, and the availability of goods and services for residents. The study's outcomes will shape the physical character of neighborhoods and the economic opportunities available to local families.
2026-05-27.how: Residents can attend the meeting on May 27, 2026, at 5:30 PM in the Council Chambers at 715 Princess Anne Street. Public comment periods are typically available during work sessions; check the agenda posted 48 hours prior for specific sign-up procedures. You may also submit written comments via email to the City Clerk or your council member before the meeting date.

2026-05-26.summary: No specific agenda items are currently published for the Fredericksburg City Council session on May 26, 2026, as the detailed agenda has not yet been released. Typically, such sessions involve the consideration of ordinances, budget approvals, or policy updates affecting city operations. 2026-05-26.why: City Council decisions directly impact local property values, zoning regulations, and the allocation of funds for public services like schools and infrastructure. Understanding the agenda helps residents anticipate changes that may affect their neighborhood's character, tax burden, and quality of life. Engaging with these decisions ensures that community priorities are reflected in municipal governance.

Attend the session at the Council Chambers to observe proceedings and submit public comments during the designated time slots. Residents may also email council members with written input regarding specific agenda items once they are available.
